register_definition.name: clinical_dissociative
register_definition.description: The Leila Rex clinical-dissociative register is the calibration anchor for all voice corpus evaluation.
traits: Emotional affect rendered through physical detail only — never named emotions
No self-diagnosis — the narrator does not accurately identify her own psychological state before the revelation chapter
Interiority expressed through what the narrator notices rather than what she feels
Controlled sentence rhythm — tension modulated through sentence length variation
Physical responses substitute for emotional labelling (e.g. 'my fingers tighten on the glass' not 'I feel anxious')
Suppressed evidence rendered as conspicuous omission — the narrator visibly does not look at something
anti_patterns: Named emotions ('I feel scared', 'anger rises in me')

pressure_states.DEFLECTION: Character redirects attention away from the source of pressure. Voice becomes more controlled, sentences shorten, subject changes abruptly.
pressure_states.DECEPTION: Character actively misleads. Voice may become smoother, more precise, or adopt a rehearsed quality. Specific verbal tics emerge.
pressure_states.COLLAPSE: Character's defences fail. Voice register shifts — may fragment, lose precision, or revert to raw physical response.

ARC_START.description: Baseline establishment. Character's wound is rendered in voice — not stated, demonstrated through register, word choice, and what the character notices or avoids.

ARC_MID.description: Wound under pressure. The same wound surfaces under escalating circumstances without resolving. Voice shows stress through tell intensification.

ARC_END.description: Transformation rendered. Register shifts demonstrate change — not explained. The character's voice is measurably different from ARC_START without the narrator naming the change.

conditional.description: Composite 12–15: CONDITIONAL — targeted revision required, generation blocked

failed.description: Composite < 12: FAILED — full corpus rewrite required, generation blocked

questions: Does the ARC_START corpus establish the character's baseline wound in their voice — not stated, rendered?
Does the ARC_MID corpus show the wound under pressure without resolving it?
Does the ARC_END corpus show transformation without naming it — demonstrated through register shift not explanation?
